mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by retail sales (RSAFS), signals robust consumer demand which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with RSAFS monthly change is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over a rolling 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5% over three months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE trigger condition holds, the 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 3% and housing starts fall below 1.2 million annualized, the joint signal indicates tight monetary policy constricting housing investment, which depresses economic growth and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (p>0.10), or the signal produces fewer than 10 valid episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime where CPI year-over-year exceeds 5%, further increases in the federal funds rate are more contractionary as they intensify the real interest rate burden, reducing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with INDPRO monthly change is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over a rolling 2-year window, or the signal is not robust after controlling for other factors.

mechanism_narrative: A large negative monthly change in nonfarm payrolls below -200,000 signals immediate labor market distress that erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS drops below -200k, the 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (p>0.10), or positive returns occur in more than 40% of events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and industrial production signal a broad-based weakening in economic activity, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falling and INDPRO 3-month moving average falling
failure_condition: When both series are declining (3-month MA decreasing), the 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the combined signal does not outperform a single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor market downturn defined by unemployment above 6%, an unexpected increase in retail sales signals a temporary consumption boost that is not sustainable, leading to a negative market reaction as it delays necessary adjustments, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 6%, positive RSAFS surprises (monthly change > 0) are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns (p>0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.
